test.name: Scenario: MCP Server SSE Connection Setup

kw.name: Connect To MCP SSE
Get Session Id From Sse
kw.doc: Connects to the SSE endpoint, reads the first 'endpoint' event, and returns the sessionId. Note: This closes the connection after getting the ID, which is fine for the current simple tool-call tests if the server allows session persistence for a short window or if the session isn't strictly bound to the socket's lifecycle (though in our current Echo implementation it is).
Establishes an SSE connection and returns the session ID.

kw.name: Should Not Be Empty
kw.doc: Verifies that the given item is not empty.

test.doc: Verify that the MCP server accepts SSE connections and returns a session ID.

test.name: Scenario: Full Project CRUD Lifecycle

kw.name: Connect To MCP SSE
Get Session Id From Sse
kw.doc: Connects to the SSE endpoint, reads the first 'endpoint' event, and returns the sessionId. Note: This closes the connection after getting the ID, which is fine for the current simple tool-call tests if the server allows session persistence for a short window or if the session isn't strictly bound to the socket's lifecycle (though in our current Echo implementation it is).
Establishes an SSE connection and returns the session ID.

kw.name: Create Dictionary
kw.doc: Creates and returns a dictionary based on the given ``items``.

kw.name: Create Dictionary
kw.doc: Creates and returns a dictionary based on the given ``items``.

kw.name: Create Dictionary
kw.doc: Creates and returns a dictionary based on the given ``items``.

kw.name: Set To Dictionary
kw.doc: Adds the given ``key_value_pairs`` and/or ``items`` to the ``dictionary``.

kw.name: POST
kw.msg: POST Request : url=http://localhost:8080/message?sessionId=9343c9a1-89fa-4657-a97a-dbb6130b43f9 path_url=/message?sessionId=9343c9a1-89fa-4657-a97a-dbb6130b43f9 headers={'User-Agent': 'python-requests/2.33.1', 'Accept-Encoding': 'gzip, deflate', 'Accept': '*/*', 'Connection': 'keep-alive', 'Content-Length': '168', 'Content-Type': 'application/json'} body=b'{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": "1", "method": "tools/call", "params": {"name": "create_project", "arguments": {"name": "Test Project", "description": "Initial description"}}}'
kw.doc: Sends a POST request.

kw.name: Set Variable
kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

kw.name: Evaluate
kw.doc: Evaluates the given expression in Python and returns the result.

kw.name: Set Variable
kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

kw.name: Should Be Equal
kw.doc: Fails if the given objects are unequal.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
Set To Dictionary
kw.doc: Adds the given ``key_value_pairs`` and/or ``items`` to the ``dictionary``.
Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.doc: Sends a POST request.

test.name: Scenario: Full Document CRUD Lifecycle

kw.name: Connect To MCP SSE
Get Session Id From Sse
kw.doc: Connects to the SSE endpoint, reads the first 'endpoint' event, and returns the sessionId. Note: This closes the connection after getting the ID, which is fine for the current simple tool-call tests if the server allows session persistence for a short window or if the session isn't strictly bound to the socket's lifecycle (though in our current Echo implementation it is).
Establishes an SSE connection and returns the session ID.

kw.name: Connect To MCP SSE
Get Session Id From Sse
kw.doc: Connects to the SSE endpoint, reads the first 'endpoint' event, and returns the sessionId. Note: This closes the connection after getting the ID, which is fine for the current simple tool-call tests if the server allows session persistence for a short window or if the session isn't strictly bound to the socket's lifecycle (though in our current Echo implementation it is).
Establishes an SSE connection and returns the session ID.

test.name: Scenario: Full Task CRUD Lifecycle

kw.name: Connect To MCP SSE
Get Session Id From Sse
kw.doc: Connects to the SSE endpoint, reads the first 'endpoint' event, and returns the sessionId. Note: This closes the connection after getting the ID, which is fine for the current simple tool-call tests if the server allows session persistence for a short window or if the session isn't strictly bound to the socket's lifecycle (though in our current Echo implementation it is).
Establishes an SSE connection and returns the session ID.

kw.name: Evaluate
kw.doc: Evaluates the given expression in Python and returns the result.

kw.name: Should Be Equal
kw.doc: Fails if the given objects are unequal.

kw.name: Create Dictionary
kw.doc: Creates and returns a dictionary based on the given ``items``.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
Set To Dictionary
kw.doc: Adds the given ``key_value_pairs`` and/or ``items`` to the ``dictionary``.
Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.

kw.name: Create Dictionary
kw.doc: Creates and returns a dictionary based on the given ``items``.

kw.name: Set To Dictionary
kw.doc: Adds the given ``key_value_pairs`` and/or ``items`` to the ``dictionary``.

kw.name: POST

kw.name: Evaluate
kw.doc: Evaluates the given expression in Python and returns the result.

kw.name: Set Variable
kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

kw.name: Set Variable
kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

kw.doc: Fails if the given condition is not true.

test.doc: Create, read, update, list and delete a task via MCP tools.

suite.stat: REQ001 agent board mcp.US001 mcp server setup
REQ001 agent board mcp.US002 project crud
REQ001 agent board mcp.US003 document crud
REQ001 agent board mcp.US004 user story crud
REQ001 agent board mcp.US005 task crud
